title: What Relationships Actually Are
body:
Relationships are structured patterns of mutual dependence. They include romantic bonds, friendships, families, teams, and communities, all of which ask people to coordinate needs, expectations, and emotions over time.

The relationship layer is where attachment, communication, and trust become visible in action. A healthy relationship is not defined by the absence of disagreement, but by the presence of safety, honesty, and a shared willingness to repair when things go wrong.

Much of what people call chemistry is really a mix of recognition, timing, values, and the degree to which two people can tolerate and understand each other under pressure.
keyPoints: Relationships are systems of repeated exchange, not one-off feelings
Attachment and trust shape how people handle closeness and distance
Healthy bonds depend on repair, not just attraction
Boundaries keep care from turning into resentment
Relationship quality improves when people can name needs clearly
furtherReading: Attached by Amir Levine and Rachel Heller
The Seven Principles for Making Marriage Work by John Gottman
Hold Me Tight by Sue Johnson
